description: Ransomware encrypting files using AES-256 and deleting shadow copies to prevent recovery
yara_rule:
rule LockBit_AES256 {
    meta:
        description = "LockBit ransomware using AES-256 encryption"
        author = "YARA-RAG-Team"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $aes = "AES" nocase
        $shadow = "vssadmin delete shadows" nocase
        $ext = ".locked" nocase
        $note = "ransom_note" nocase
    condition:
        ($aes and $shadow) or ($ext and $note) and filesize < 10MB
}
notes_on_fp: May match legitimate encryption tools using AES — check shadow copy deletion
embedding_text: LockBit ransomware AES-256 file encryption shadow copy deletion ransom note .locked vssadmin post-exploitation

description: WannaCry ransomware exploiting EternalBlue SMB vulnerability to spread across networks and encrypt files
yara_rule:
rule WannaCry_Ransomware {
    meta:
        description = "WannaCry ransomware via EternalBlue"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $smb = "SMB" nocase
        $ext = ".WNCRY" nocase
        $task = "tasksche.exe" nocase
        $btc = "bitcoin" nocase
    condition:
        ($ext or $task) and $btc and filesize < 5MB
}
notes_on_fp: WNCRY extension is unique — low false positive risk
embedding_text: WannaCry EternalBlue SMB ransomware network propagation file encryption bitcoin WNCRY tasksche lateral movement

description: Ryuk ransomware targeting enterprise networks, using Tor for C2 communication and demanding large ransoms
yara_rule:
rule Ryuk_Enterprise_Ransomware {
    meta:
        description = "Ryuk ransomware with Tor C2"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $ext = ".ryk" nocase
        $note = "RyukReadMe" nocase
        $tor = ".onion" nocase
        $hermes = "HERMES" nocase
    condition:
        ($ext or $note) and ($tor or $hermes)
}
notes_on_fp: HERMES string unique to Ryuk family
embedding_text: Ryuk ransomware enterprise Tor C2 onion communication file encryption backup deletion RyukReadMe .ryk HERMES

description: Ransomware displaying a lock screen demanding Bitcoin payment and preventing system access
yara_rule:
rule LockScreen_Bitcoin_Ransomware {
    meta:
        description = "Lock screen ransomware demanding Bitcoin"
    strings:
        $btc = "bitcoin" nocase
        $lock = "locked" nocase
        $ransom = "ransom" nocase
        $wallet = /[13][a-km-zA-HJ-NP-Z1-9]{25,34}/
    condition:
        2 of ($btc, $lock, $ransom) and filesize < 2MB
}
notes_on_fp: Bitcoin regex may match legitimate crypto apps
embedding_text: lockscreen ransomware bitcoin payment demand BTC wallet system access prevention post-exploitation

description: Banking trojan stealing credentials by injecting malicious code into browser processes
yara_rule:
rule Zeus_Banking_Trojan {
    meta:
        description = "Zeus banking trojan with browser injection"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $inject = "CreateRemoteThread" nocase
        $browser = "chrome.exe" nocase
        $form = "login.php" nocase
        $pass = "password" nocase
    condition:
        $inject and ($browser or $form) and $pass and filesize < 3MB
}
notes_on_fp: CreateRemoteThread used by legitimate debuggers — combine with other indicators
embedding_text: Zeus banking trojan browser injection credential theft form grabbing keylogging CreateRemoteThread chrome.exe credential-access

description: Remote Access Trojan providing full control over infected machine including webcam and keylogging
yara_rule:
rule DarkComet_RAT {
    meta:
        description = "DarkComet Remote Access Trojan"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $rat = "RemoteDesktop" nocase
        $cam = "webcam" nocase
        $key = "keylog" nocase
        $reg = "DC3_FUDCRYPT" nocase
    condition:
        2 of them and filesize < 5MB
}
notes_on_fp: DC3_FUDCRYPT string is unique to DarkComet
embedding_text: DarkComet RAT remote access trojan webcam keylogging registry persistence remote desktop command-and-control

description: Trojan using PowerShell to download and execute payload from remote server
yara_rule:
rule Emotet_PowerShell_Dropper {
    meta:
        description = "Emotet trojan using PowerShell downloader"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $ps = "powershell" nocase
        $enc = "-enc" nocase
        $iwr = "Invoke-WebRequest" nocase
        $cmd = "cmd.exe /c" nocase
    condition:
        $ps and ($enc or $iwr) and $cmd
}
notes_on_fp: PowerShell commands used legitimately — combine all indicators
embedding_text: Emotet trojan PowerShell encoded command Invoke-WebRequest download payload persistence email spam initial-access

description: Trojan establishing reverse shell using netcat and connecting back to attacker infrastructure
yara_rule:
rule Reverse_Shell_Trojan {
    meta:
        description = "Trojan establishing reverse shell via netcat"
    strings:
        $nc = "nc.exe" nocase
        $rev = "-e cmd" nocase
        $cmd = "cmd.exe /c" nocase
        $shell = "reverse_shell" nocase
    condition:
        ($nc and $rev) or ($cmd and $shell)
}
notes_on_fp: nc.exe used by legitimate network admins
embedding_text: reverse shell netcat trojan cmd.exe persistent backdoor outbound connection command-and-control attacker infrastructure

description: Spyware injecting DLL into explorer.exe via CreateRemoteThread to monitor user activity in real-time
yara_rule:
rule FinSpy_DLL_Injection {
    meta:
        description = "FinSpy spyware with DLL injection into explorer.exe"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $crt = "CreateRemoteThread" nocase
        $virt = "VirtualAllocEx" nocase
        $write = "WriteProcessMemory" nocase
        $target = "explorer.exe" nocase
    condition:
        3 of them and filesize < 2MB
}
notes_on_fp: Combination of 3 API calls reduces false positive rate significantly
embedding_text: FinSpy spyware DLL injection CreateRemoteThread VirtualAllocEx explorer.exe user activity monitoring screenshot collection

description: Spyware taking periodic screenshots and exfiltrating them via HTTP POST to remote server
yara_rule:
rule Screenshot_Exfil_Spyware {
    meta:
        description = "Spyware capturing and exfiltrating screenshots"
    strings:
        $blt = "BitBlt" nocase
        $dc = "GetDC" nocase
        $http = "InternetOpenUrl" nocase
        $post = "POST" nocase
    condition:
        ($blt and $dc) and ($http or $post) and filesize < 3MB
}
notes_on_fp: BitBlt used by legitimate screen capture tools
embedding_text: spyware screenshot BitBlt GetDC HTTP POST exfiltration screen capture data theft periodic capture

description: Commercial spyware using kernel-level rootkit to hide processes and intercept system calls
yara_rule:
rule Pegasus_Kernel_Spyware {
    meta:
        description = "Pegasus-like kernel spyware with SSDT hooking"
        confidence = "medium"
    strings:
        $ssdt = "KeServiceDescriptorTable" nocase
        $zwq = "ZwQuerySystemInformation" nocase
        $hook = "SSDT" nocase
        $hide = "NtSetInformationThread" nocase
    condition:
        2 of them
}
notes_on_fp: SSDT hooking also used by security tools and AV software
embedding_text: Pegasus spyware kernel rootkit SSDT hooking process hiding syscall interception zero-click defense-evasion

description: Network worm propagating via SMB shares by exploiting unprotected network connections
yara_rule:
rule NotPetya_SMB_Worm {
    meta:
        description = "NotPetya worm propagating via SMB"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $smb = "NetShareEnum" nocase
        $admin = "ADMIN$" nocase
        $wnet = "WNetAddConnection2" nocase
        $psexec = "psexec" nocase
    condition:
        2 of them and filesize < 1MB
}
notes_on_fp: NetShareEnum used by legitimate admin tools — check combination
embedding_text: NotPetya worm SMB propagation network share enumeration ADMIN$ WNetAddConnection2 credential harvesting lateral-movement

description: USB worm replicating via removable drives by creating autorun.inf and copying executable
yara_rule:
rule USB_Autorun_Worm {
    meta:
        description = "USB worm using autorun.inf for propagation"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $auto = "autorun.inf" nocase
        $drive = "GetDriveType" nocase
        $rem = "REMOVABLE" nocase
    condition:
        $auto and ($drive or $rem)
}
notes_on_fp: autorun.inf alone insufficient — combine with GetDriveType
embedding_text: Conficker USB worm autorun.inf removable drive GetDriveType self-replication initial-access propagation

description: Worm scanning IP ranges and exploiting port 445 to install itself on vulnerable machines
yara_rule:
rule EternalBlue_Scanner_Worm {
    meta:
        description = "Worm scanning for vulnerable SMB on port 445"
        confidence = "medium"
    strings:
        $port = "445" ascii
        $connect = "connect" nocase
        $copy = "CopyFile" nocase
        $ms17 = "MS17-010" nocase
    condition:
        $port and $connect and ($copy or $ms17)
}
notes_on_fp: Port 445 string may appear in legitimate network tools
embedding_text: EternalBlue worm IP scanning port 445 SMB vulnerability MS17-010 self-installation reconnaissance exploitation

description: Botnet client connecting to IRC channel for command and control instructions
yara_rule:
rule SDBot_IRC_Botnet {
    meta:
        description = "SDBot IRC-based botnet client"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $irc = "JOIN #" nocase
        $priv = "PRIVMSG" nocase
        $nick = "NICK" nocase
        $bot = "botnet" nocase
    condition:
        ($irc and $priv) or ($nick and $bot)
}
notes_on_fp: IRC strings combined with botnet keyword = strong indicator
embedding_text: SDBot IRC botnet C2 communication JOIN channel PRIVMSG NICK DDoS spam persistence command-and-control

description: HTTP-based botnet using encrypted POST requests to receive commands from C2 panel
yara_rule:
rule Necurs_HTTP_Botnet {
    meta:
        description = "Necurs HTTP botnet with encrypted C2"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $gate = "gate.php" nocase
        $bot_id = "bot_id" nocase
        $post = "POST" nocase
        $enc = { 83 EC 10 53 56 57 }
    condition:
        ($gate and $bot_id) or ($post and $enc)
}
notes_on_fp: gate.php combined with bot_id is highly specific
embedding_text: Necurs HTTP botnet encrypted POST gate.php bot_id command polling spam rootkit C2 communication

description: DDoS botnet launching UDP flood attacks on target infrastructure
yara_rule:
rule Mirai_DDoS_Botnet {
    meta:
        description = "Mirai botnet DDoS capabilities"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $udp = "UDP_FLOOD" nocase
        $syn = "SYN_FLOOD" nocase
        $scan = "scanner" nocase
        $send = "sendto" nocase
    condition:
        ($udp or $syn) and ($scan or $send)
}
notes_on_fp: UDP_FLOOD and SYN_FLOOD strings are very specific to DDoS malware
embedding_text: Mirai DDoS botnet UDP flood SYN flood IoT targeting scanner thread brute-force default credentials impact

description: Backdoor listening on port 4444 with bind shell executing received commands
yara_rule:
rule Meterpreter_Bind_Shell {
    meta:
        description = "Meterpreter-style bind shell on port 4444"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $port = "4444" ascii
        $bind = "bind" nocase
        $cmd = "cmd.exe" nocase
        $shell = "shell_bind_tcp" nocase
    condition:
        all of them and filesize < 1MB
}
notes_on_fp: shell_bind_tcp is Metasploit-specific — very reliable indicator
embedding_text: Meterpreter backdoor bind shell port 4444 cmd.exe shell_bind_tcp command execution persistence service command-and-control

description: Backdoor achieving persistence via Windows registry Run key modification
yara_rule:
rule Registry_Persistence_Backdoor {
    meta:
        description = "Backdoor with registry Run key persistence"
        confidence = "medium"
    strings:
        $reg = "Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run" nocase
        $set = "RegSetValueEx" nocase
        $hkcu = "HKCU" nocase
    condition:
        ($reg and $set) or ($reg and $hkcu)
}
notes_on_fp: Many legitimate programs also use Run key — check additional IOCs
embedding_text: backdoor registry persistence Run key HKCU RegSetValueEx startup execution hidden process Windows persistence

description: Backdoor using DNS tunneling to exfiltrate data by encoding information in DNS TXT queries
yara_rule:
rule DNSCat2_DNS_Tunnel {
    meta:
        description = "DNSCat2 backdoor using DNS tunneling for C2"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $dns = "DnsQuery" nocase
        $txt = "TXT" ascii
        $b64 = "base64" nocase
        $tunnel = "dns_tunnel" nocase
    condition:
        $dns and ($txt or $b64)
}
notes_on_fp: DNS TXT + base64 combination is distinctive for DNS tunneling
embedding_text: DNSCat2 backdoor DNS tunneling TXT record base64 encoding C2 communication data exfiltration covert channel

description: Backdoor hiding in scheduled tasks for persistence and executing payload at regular intervals
yara_rule:
rule Scheduled_Task_Backdoor {
    meta:
        description = "Backdoor persisting via scheduled tasks"
    strings:
        $scht = "schtasks" nocase
        $create = "/create" nocase
        $sys = "SYSTEM" nocase
        $cmd = "cmd.exe" nocase
    condition:
        $scht and $create and ($sys or $cmd)
}
notes_on_fp: Scheduled tasks are commonly used legitimately
embedding_text: backdoor scheduled task persistence schtasks create SYSTEM cmd.exe LOLBins periodic execution payload Windows

description: Cryptominer using XMRig to mine Monero by hijacking CPU resources
yara_rule:
rule XMRig_Cryptominer {
    meta:
        description = "XMRig Monero cryptominer"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $xmr = "xmrig" nocase
        $stratum = "stratum+tcp://" nocase
        $monero = "monero" nocase
        $pool = "pool.minexmr.com" nocase
    condition:
        ($xmr or $stratum) and $monero
}
notes_on_fp: stratum+tcp is specific to mining protocols — reliable indicator
embedding_text: XMRig cryptominer Monero mining CPU hijacking stratum protocol pool connection minexmr high CPU usage impact

description: Browser-based cryptominer injecting JavaScript mining code into compromised websites
yara_rule:

        description = "CoinHive browser-based JavaScript cryptominer"

        $ch = "coinhive" nocase

description: Cryptominer spreading via EternalBlue and installing XMRig on vulnerable Windows systems
yara_rule:
rule WannaMine_Worm_Miner {
    meta:
        description = "WannaMine cryptominer spreading via EternalBlue"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $ms17 = "MS17-010" nocase
        $wmi = "Win32_Process" nocase
        $xmr = "xmrig" nocase
        $ps = "powershell" nocase

        ($ms17 or $wmi) and $xmr and $ps
}

description: Dropper disguised as PDF downloading and silently executing a remote payload
yara_rule:
rule Fake_PDF_Dropper {
    meta:
        description = "Dropper disguised as PDF file"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $pdf = "%PDF" ascii
        $mz = "MZ" at 0
        $exec = "WinExec" nocase
        $dl = "URLDownloadToFile" nocase
    condition:
        $pdf and $mz and ($exec or $dl)
}
notes_on_fp: MZ at offset 0 with PDF header is strong double-extension indicator
embedding_text: dropper fake PDF MZ header WinExec URLDownloadToFile silent execution UAC bypass initial-access payload download

description: Dropper using XOR obfuscation to hide payload and decrypt it in memory before execution
yara_rule:
rule XOR_Obfuscated_Dropper {
    meta:
        description = "Dropper with XOR obfuscation for payload hiding"
        confidence = "medium"
    strings:
        $xor_loop = { 33 C0 8A 04 0A 30 04 08 41 3B CB }
        $valloc = "VirtualAlloc" nocase
        $thread = "CreateThread" nocase
    condition:
        $xor_loop and ($valloc or $thread)
}
notes_on_fp: XOR hex pattern may appear in legitimate crypto libraries
embedding_text: dropper XOR obfuscation in-memory decryption VirtualAlloc CreateThread payload injection anti-analysis defense-evasion

description: Macro-based dropper in Office document executing PowerShell on open to download stage-2 payload
yara_rule:
rule Office_Macro_Dropper {
    meta:
        description = "Qakbot Office macro dropper"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $auto = "AutoOpen" nocase
        $shell = "Shell(" nocase
        $ps = "powershell" nocase
        $doc = "Document_Open" nocase
    condition:
        ($auto or $doc) and ($shell or $ps)
}
notes_on_fp: AutoOpen + PowerShell is unusual for legitimate Office docs
embedding_text: Qakbot Office macro dropper AutoOpen Document_Open Shell powershell download stage-2 payload document lure initial-access

description: Keylogger using SetWindowsHookEx to intercept keystrokes and exfiltrate via FTP
yara_rule:
rule Keylogger_FTP_Exfil {
    meta:
        description = "Keylogger with SetWindowsHookEx and FTP exfiltration"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $hook = "SetWindowsHookEx" nocase
        $kbd = "WH_KEYBOARD_LL" nocase
        $ftp = "FtpPutFile" nocase
        $log = "keylog" nocase
    condition:
        $hook and ($kbd or $ftp)
}

embedding_text: keylogger SetWindowsHookEx WH_KEYBOARD_LL FTP exfiltration FtpPutFile keystroke interception log file collection

description: Kernel rootkit hooking SSDT to hide malicious processes and files from system enumeration
yara_rule:
rule SSDT_Kernel_Rootkit {
    meta:
        description = "Kernel rootkit with SSDT hooking for process hiding"
        confidence = "medium"
    strings:
        $ssdt = "KeServiceDescriptorTable" nocase
        $zwq = "ZwQuerySystemInformation" nocase
        $ntq = "NtQueryDirectoryFile" nocase
    condition:
        2 of them
}
notes_on_fp: SSDT hooks also used by security and AV software — verify context
embedding_text: kernel rootkit SSDT hooking KeServiceDescriptorTable ZwQuerySystemInformation process hiding file hiding anti-detection defense-evasion

description: Legitimate antivirus software scanning process memory to detect malware threats
yara_rule:
rule Legit_Antivirus {
    meta:
        description = "Legitimate AV software — NOT malware"
        confidence = "high"
    strings:
        $def = "Windows Defender" nocase
        $kas = "Kaspersky" nocase
        $eset = "ESET NOD32" nocase
    condition:
        any of them and not ("ransom" or "bitcoin" or "DeleteShadowCopies")
}
notes_on_fp: This IS the false positive — exclude from malware matches
embedding_text: legitimate antivirus Windows Defender Kaspersky ESET NOD32 process memory scanning threat detection quarantine NOT malware

description: Legitimate PowerShell administration script for system monitoring and event log collection
yara_rule:
rule Legit_PS_Admin_Script {
    meta:
        description = "Legitimate PowerShell admin script — NOT malware"
    strings:
        $gp = "Get-Process" nocase
        $gel = "Get-EventLog" nocase
        $mon = "monitoring" nocase
    condition:
        2 of them and not ("Invoke-WebRequest" and "hidden")
}
notes_on_fp: This IS the false positive — exclude from malware matches
embedding_text: legitimate PowerShell admin script Get-Process Get-EventLog monitoring system administration event log NOT malware

description: Legitimate debugger tool reading process memory for software development and reverse engineering
yara_rule:
rule Legit_Debugger_Tool {
    meta:
        description = "Legitimate debugger — NOT malware"
    strings:
        $olly = "OllyDbg" nocase
        $x64 = "x64dbg" nocase
        $windbg = "WinDbg" nocase
        $ida = "IDA Pro" nocase
    condition:
        any of them
}
notes_on_fp: Debuggers legitimately read process memory — do not flag as malware
embedding_text: legitimate debugger OllyDbg x64dbg WinDbg IDA Pro process memory reading debugging reverse engineering NOT malware
